title_en: “What can we do about it?” The value of creativity knowledge in designing for regenerative transitions
title_zh: “我们能做些什么？“创造力知识在设计再生过渡中的价值
description_en:
Lara Rodgett Exploring how the language of England’s climate policy has evolved from 1961 to the present, Lara Rodgett presents ‘What if we worked with Nature?,’ a visual interpretation of her PhD research. It examines the relationality of climate policy and how we might guide the future by understanding the past. A huge part of climate action is the accessibility and understanding of knowledge, and if we cannot connect or relate the information to its nature-based context, is climate policy fulfilling its purpose? Contact Laral.rodgett1220231@arts.ac.uk 
Instagram

title_zh: 与大海约会
description_en: The ocean has been trying to communicate its pain for years, through rising tides, dwindling fish stocks, and coral bleaching; but humanity hasn’t always listened. ‘A Date with the Ocean’ is an unscripted experiment that prompts its participants to consider how they could forge a happy, harmonious and symbiotic relationship with the Ocean.

title_en: A Lifestyle Magazine For Sensitive People
title_zh: 适合敏感人士的生活方式杂志
description_en: This project is to create a magazine for Highly Sensitive People(HSPs) who need holistic guidance on well-being and test its relevance and effectiveness through the Focus Group. The research started with the main question, "What does a magazine which helps highly Sensitive People look like?" Following that, the essential findings derived from psychology and publishing research were that sensitive people, particularly HSPs, want a publication that provides a sense of calmness, information about hypersensitivity, and community.

title_en: A Practice-Led Research: How AI-Generated Art Decenters Authorship in NFT Marketplaces from a Posthumanism Perspective
title_zh: 以实践为主导的研究：人工智能生成的艺术如何从后人文主义的角度来看待NFT市场的作者身份
description_en: Zihan Wang Zihan Wang’s research examines how AI-generated art (AIGA) on NFT platforms challenges traditional authorship by exploring hybrid creative subjects, distributed generative logic, and decentralized creative contexts. Contributing to posthumanist art theory, it offers guidance for artists adapting to decentralized ecosystems, highlighting the dissolution of the "author-consumer" binary. Contact Zihanz.wang0720212@arts.ac.ukInstagram
description_zh: Zihan Wang Zihan Wang’s research examines how AI-generated art (AIGA) on NFT platforms challenges traditional authorship by exploring hybrid creative subjects, distributed generative logic, and decentralized creative contexts. title_en: Afterlife: how can the existing LCC buildings make their mark?
title_zh: 来世：现有的LCC建筑如何才能留下自己的印记？
description_en:
Natalie Robinson These papier-mâché sculptures imagine possibilities for the future of LCC’s Workshop Block’s external skin. ‘Rupture’ is a full-scale re-creation of a damaged section of the south-facing wall; “broken” by time, it is forced apart by carbon escaping from the dying building. ‘Proposal for a careful mending of a retained façade’ considers the wall facing Newington Butts, acknowledging and celebrating the imperfection of a fracture as part of the building’s history. Contact Natalien.robinson1020191@arts.ac.uk  
Website | Instagram

title_zh: Anthrophony
description_en: Anthrophony is a multi-sensory installation, depicting the impact of sound pollution in the deep sea. Using cyanotype photography, we captured sound waves in water, showcasing varying intensities of ripples on a series of cyanotype booklets, allowing viewers to read while observing the images.
description_zh: Anthrophony is a multi-sensory installation, depicting the impact of sound pollution in the deep sea. Using cyanotype photography, we captured sound waves in water, showcasing varying intensities of ripples on a series of cyanotype booklets, allowing viewers to read while observing the images.

title_zh: 无形资产地图集
description_en: In Atlas of Intangibles, Pandurangan uses score-based data walks in London to record traces of interactions between humans, nature and architecture. The project explores how our personal memories of a place occur from fleeting sensory experiences.
description_zh: In Atlas of Intangibles, Pandurangan uses score-based data walks in London to record traces of interactions between humans, nature and architecture. The project explores how our personal memories of a place occur from fleeting sensory experiences.

title_zh: 等候房间
description_en: Born from a piece of automatic writing, with the title being a play-on-words for asking what you (the audience) are really waiting for, this original sound-art work aims to transport audiences into altered states of consciousness and extract the truest forms of memory and emotion. Whether these states are positive or negative is within the receivers' hands, the work acting as a vessel for outbursts of true emotional and physical response. Combining mediums of cinematography, slow-cinema and colourful synthesis, the work aims to influence the space and its documentation through the ambiguous serenity of the visuals & sonics.

title_en: Becoming through Marginalia: Iterative Annotations in Reading
title_zh: 通过Marginalia ：阅读中的迭代注释
description_en:
Ziqi Guo Every day I read the advertisements flickering on television screens, the names of bus stops, the endless messages I scroll through on my phone. Why read a book? Why must it be a book? Some books cut into my hidden thoughts with their sharp edges. Some books leave me shaken and jealous. And so, and so, I retaliate. I want to write. I want to write in the margins. I want to write over all the great works until every word is covered, layered beyond recognition. This is my reading. Contact Ziqi z.guo0220231@arts.ac.uk  
Instagram

description_en: Yu Feng A Comic Workshop Guide by Yu Feng invites Chinese immigrant children to co-create comics, weaving memory, imagination, and heritage. Through storytelling, children are encouraged to reflect on identity and belonging while drawing out the power of their voices to echo beyond the pages. Contact Yuy.feng1220231@arts.ac.uk
description_zh: Yu Feng A Comic Workshop Guide by Yu Feng invites Chinese immigrant children to co-create comics, weaving memory, imagination, and heritage. Through storytelling, children are encouraged to reflect on identity and belonging while drawing out the power of their voices to echo beyond the pages. Contact Yuy.feng1220231@arts.ac.uk

title_en: Breaking Gender Stereotypes in Chinese Shaonian Comics
title_zh: 打破中国邵年漫画中的性别刻板印象
description_en: Daishan Werther Despite global developments in gender equality, Shaonian comics—Chinese teenage adventure comics—are dominated by male protagonists and gendered stereotypes. Through her comic Endless, Daishan Werther imagines an alternative in which the strength of female characters and the gentleness of male characters can be more powerfully depicted. Contact DaishanInstagram | Rednote: @wertherh
description_zh: Daishan Werther Despite global developments in gender equality, Shaonian comics—Chinese teenage adventure comics—are dominated by male protagonists and gendered stereotypes. Through her comic Endless, Daishan Werther imagines an alternative in which the strength of female characters and the gentleness of male characters can be more powerfully depicted. Contact DaishanInstagram | Rednote: @wertherh

title_zh: 呼吸清新空气Name
description_en: In this bright and colourful animation, Drishti Kosambia helps us appreciate the beauty of our surroundings — a creative, innovative approach that inspires new ways of seeing and learning.
description_zh: In this bright and colourful animation, Drishti Kosambia helps us appreciate the beauty of our surroundings — a creative, innovative approach that inspires new ways of seeing and learning.

title_en: Bridging Transitions: Co-creating Comics with Chinese Immigrant Families in London for Intergenerational Dialogue, Heritage, and Identity
title_zh: 过渡桥梁：与伦敦的中国移民家庭共同创作漫画，促进代际对话、遗产和身份认同
description_en: Yu Feng Yu Feng presents comics from ongoing, practice-based research with British Chinese children aged six to ten in London, developed through four themed workshops. Parent interviews are transformed into short prompts, which children answer through drawing and storytelling. Mapped as a network, the comics unfold as turn-by-turn “ping-pong” exchanges negotiating everyday heritage, migration memory, and identity, shaped by humour, objects, metaphor, partnership, and bilingual design toward repair, reframing, and shared belonging. Contact Yuy.feng1220231@arts.ac.uk
description_zh: Yu Feng Yu Feng presents comics from ongoing, practice-based research with British Chinese children aged six to ten in London, developed through four themed workshops. Parent interviews are transformed into short prompts, which children answer through drawing and storytelling. Mapped as a network, the comics unfold as turn-by-turn “ping-pong” exchanges negotiating everyday heritage, migration memory, and identity, shaped by humour, objects, metaphor, partnership, and bilingual design toward repair, reframing, and shared belonging. Contact Yuy.feng1220231@arts.ac.uk

title_zh: Ziyin Zeng的Bubble Land
description_en: Dive into the enchanting realm of 'Bubble Land,' where self-seeking meets redemption in a whimsical journey. This project, a fusion of art and psychology, explores the profound impact of positive mental cues and the law of attraction.
description_zh: Dive into the enchanting realm of 'Bubble Land,' where self-seeking meets redemption in a whimsical journey. This project, a fusion of art and psychology, explores the profound impact of positive mental cues and the law of attraction.

title_en: Co-Creating a VR Monument of Counter-Discourse: Storytelling, Collective Experience, and Narrative Resistance in the Chinese COVID-19 Crisis
title_zh: 共同打造反话语的虚拟现实纪念碑：中国新冠肺炎危机中的故事讲述、集体体验和叙事抵抗
description_en: Yicen (Aziel) Liu Reflecting on firsthand experiences from the Chinese COVID-19 lockdown, Yicen (Aziel) Liu uses VR as a mode of collective storytelling. This early model for a larger VR monument presents anonymised personal accounts as digital fragments viewers are invited to interact with through hand gestures. Liu explores how VR interactions shape how experiences are seen and explored. Contact Yicenhazielliu.az@gmail.comWebsite

Liu explores how VR interactions shape how experiences are seen and explored. Contact Yicenhazielliu.az@gmail.comWebsite

title_zh: 代码刺绣
description_en: Victoria Schlienkamp (LCC) and Dieter Vlasich (CSM) collaborated on a project that merges traditional craft with generative code, showing how cross-disciplinary partnerships can spark imaginative new possibilities.
description_zh: Victoria Schlienkamp (LCC) and Dieter Vlasich (CSM) collaborated on a project that merges traditional craft with generative code, showing how cross-disciplinary partnerships can spark imaginative new possibilities.

title_en: Collaborative project by Wanyan W and Jingya Li
title_zh: Wanyan W和Jingya Li合作项目
description_en: This project is a 2D puzzle game with mechanics based on gravity balls and sounds based on the Chinese pentatonic scale and Chinese instruments. In this game, the player controls the direction of the ball by controlling the rotation of the platform and in the process, the player is given a Chinese rhythmic melody. Based on a random sound effect mechanism, the player can listen to a different melody each time.The designers added other elements, such as Chinese paper cutting and Chinese mythology, to the level design to make the game more interesting.
description_zh: This project is a 2D puzzle game with mechanics based on gravity balls and sounds based on the Chinese pentatonic scale and Chinese instruments. In this game, the player controls the direction of the ball by controlling the rotation of the platform and in the process, the player is given a Chinese rhythmic melody. Based on a random sound effect mechanism, the player can listen to a different melody each time.The designers added other elements, such as Chinese paper cutting and Chinese mythology, to the level design to make the game more interesting.

title_en: Comic work: Breaking gender stereotypes in Chinese Shaonian comics
title_zh: 漫画作品：打破中国韶宁漫画中的性别刻板印象
description_en: Daishan (Werther) Xu In contemporary mainstream Chinese comics aimed at teenagers, visual narratives surrounding female characters, particularly through costume and character design, are rarely neutral. Daishan (Werther) Xu invites audiences to rethink habitual ways of seeing in comics by positioning male characters as the objects of the gaze.  Contact Daishan Instagram
description_zh: Daishan (Werther) Xu In contemporary mainstream Chinese comics aimed at teenagers, visual narratives surrounding female characters, particularly through costume and character design, are rarely neutral. Daishan (Werther) Xu invites audiences to rethink habitual ways of seeing in comics by positioning male characters as the objects of the gaze.  Contact Daishan Instagram

title_en: Comics Under Pressure: Creative Navigation and Political Expression in Russian Comics
title_zh: 压力下的漫画：俄罗斯漫画中的创意导航和政治表达
description_en: Anna Slepneva Anna Slepneva explores how Russian comics artists navigate restrictions through metaphor, satire, and autobiography. Featuring works from her research alongside artistic responses, it examines visual storytelling as a tool for self-censorship and subtle critique in politically precarious contexts. Contact AnnaWebsite
description_zh: Anna Slepneva Anna Slepneva explores how Russian comics artists navigate restrictions through metaphor, satire, and autobiography. Featuring works from her research alongside artistic responses, it examines visual storytelling as a tool for self-censorship and subtle critique in politically precarious contexts. Contact AnnaWebsite

title_zh: 沉思聆听
description_en: Powell's A line disappeared by walking traces the presence of footsteps throughout an hour-long walking meditation. Powell utilises a ground-level microphone to render the physical line walked into a sonic one.
description_zh: Powell's A line disappeared by walking traces the presence of footsteps throughout an hour-long walking meditation. Powell utilises a ground-level microphone to render the physical line walked into a sonic one.

title_en: Culture Without Borders: Where Walls Speak, Street Art Connects
title_zh: 文化无国界：墙壁说话的地方，街头艺术连接
description_en: Sylvia Albert-Vogl Sylvia Albert-Vogl presents a gateway to cultural embodiment via VR, experienced through street art, heritage, and travel narratives. As conflict-driven media fuels news avoidance, cultural narratives are often marginalised. Here, Albert-Vogl invites you to immerse yourself in the intersection of art, photography, and technology and explore how it shapes your sense of presence. Contact SylviaLinkedIn

title_zh: 尊敬的Dalyana ：
description_en: The project "Dear Dalyana" highlights the importance of preserving ancestral heritage, showing how our students celebrate cultural diversity in ways that are inclusive and approachable.
description_zh: The project "Dear Dalyana" highlights the importance of preserving ancestral heritage, showing how our students celebrate cultural diversity in ways that are inclusive and approachable.

title_zh: 深海调度
description_en: Deep Sea Dispatch is a story-based adventure game set at three ocean depths. The characters deliver packages through the ocean from the Antarctic sea ice, where phytoplankton is consumed to great depths and where marine snow falls. The bespoke soundtrack enhances the player's impression of the aquatic environments, and carefully designed characters and backgrounds create an inviting and enjoyable game.
description_zh: Deep Sea Dispatch is a story-based adventure game set at three ocean depths. The characters deliver packages through the ocean from the Antarctic sea ice, where phytoplankton is consumed to great depths and where marine snow falls. The bespoke soundtrack enhances the player's impression of the aquatic environments, and carefully designed characters and backgrounds create an inviting and enjoyable game.

title_en: Design Education for Eco-Social Justice: Developing Tangible Interventions in Learning and Teaching Approaches
title_zh: 生态社会正义设计教育：制定学习和教学方法的实际干预措施
description_en: Crystal Lezhi Zhang Observing and sketching in museum settings is central to Crystal Lezhi Zhang’s practice. Following a Fellowship in Venice and a residency in Canton, Zhang explores how Venetian glass and Cantonese porcelain narrate environmental and social realities, revealing how objects communicate stories and inform design for eco-social justice. Contact Crystall.zhang@lcc.arts.ac.uk

title_en: Designing and exploring the application of a non-digital serious game in Education for Regenerative Sustainability
title_zh: 设计和探索非数字严肃游戏在再生可持续发展教育中的应用
description_en:
Shengsheng Hu Exploring new approaches to sustainable development education within creative higher education, Shengsheng Hu’s research connects environmental theory and practice. Visitors are invited to sit down and play Hu’s board game, designed to introduce the relationship between regenerative, systemic change and individual roles within society. Combining strategy, collaboration, and surprise, it offers an accessible and engaging way to think about sustainable futures. Contact Shengshenghshengsheng710@gmail.com 
s.hu0620231@arts.ac.uk 
Instagram

title_en: Do You Know What You're Doing?
title_zh: 你知道你在干什么吗
description_en: This short film, produced by Alyssa Engmann, made with the Islington Centre for Refugees, shows how LCC students connect with local communities through social justice and activism — embodying the LCC Vision.
description_zh: This short film, produced by Alyssa Engmann, made with the Islington Centre for Refugees, shows how LCC students connect with local communities through social justice and activism — embodying the LCC Vision.

title_en: Dudel, a self-care brand for men
title_zh: Dudel ，男士自我护理品牌
description_en: The Challenge: Suicide is the single biggest killer of males under 50. Whether due to societal expectations, traditional gender roles, or both, 40% of men do not feel comfortable talking about their mental health - and so they are left to suffer in silence.The Brief: Create a self-care brand for men that breaks down the stigma of male mental health, and encourages men to process their emotions in a healthy way.My Response: Dudel is a self-care brand that utilises the mindless mindfulness of doodling to break down the barriers that make the wellness industry feel so inaccessible to men. The Dudel Diary is a wellness journal without the words, giving its users a way to work through complex ideas and confusing emotions in a way that inspires creativity and relaxation.
description_zh: The Challenge: Suicide is the single biggest killer of males under 50. Whether due to societal expectations, traditional gender roles, or both, 40% of men do not feel comfortable talking about their mental health - and so they are left to suffer in silence.The Brief: Create a self-care brand for men that breaks down the stigma of male mental health, and encourages men to process their emotions in a healthy way.My Response: Dudel is a self-care brand that utilises the mindless mindfulness of doodling to break down the barriers that make the wellness industry feel so inaccessible to men. The Dudel Diary is a wellness journal without the words, giving its users a way to work through complex ideas and confusing emotions in a way that inspires creativity and relaxation.

title_zh: 回响传统
description_en: This project examines the intricate interconnection of ancient symbolism with modern auditory encounters, drawing inspiration from the enigmatic presence of monoliths, and the enduring traditions of pagan cultures. These timeless structures, which transcend generations and cultures, serve as a profound catalyst for exploring the intersectionality between visual aesthetics and the ethereal realm of sound.
description_zh: This project examines the intricate interconnection of ancient symbolism with modern auditory encounters, drawing inspiration from the enigmatic presence of monoliths, and the enduring traditions of pagan cultures. These timeless structures, which transcend generations and cultures, serve as a profound catalyst for exploring the intersectionality between visual aesthetics and the ethereal realm of sound.

title_en: EcoGothic Art: Darkness, Agency and Time in Photography and Print
title_zh: 生态哥特式艺术：摄影和印刷中的黑暗、能动性和时间
description_en: Robyn Maclennan Transposal explores the photobook as a way of depicting the ecoGothic forest. By pulling the book apart, the obvious or “correct” path is obscured, and viewers are invited to move from image to image in any direction. The photographs have been developed using natural materials and then translated into handmade prints. Contact RobynWebsite | Instagram

title_zh: EcoSomatic身体运动
description_en: In EcoSomatic Body Movement, Seguin positions nature as a choreographer. Using Ecosomatic dance, a new area of study that links movement and healing with ecological consciousness, Seguin's hand-crafted prompt cards encourage dancers to pay attention to signals both inside and outside their bodies.

title_en: Educating Through Posters: A Decolonial and Ethical Approach to Research
title_zh: 通过海报进行教育：一种非殖民化和伦理的研究方法
description_en: Edwige SeriPositioning poster design as a pedagogical and decolonial tool, Edwige Seri examines African and Pan African ideographic systems, affirming their significance within contemporary visual culture. This exhibit presents African ideographs as living systems of philosophy, sound, and symbolic communication, repositioning communities at the centre of their own knowledge production and innovation. Contact Edwige e.seri0820191@arts.ac.uk

title_en: Education Inqilab: Content Creation for Activism
title_zh: 教育Inqilab ：激进主义的内容创作
description_en: Education Inqilab is a digital research YouTube channel hosting video essays to decolonise education, explore social justice, and empower creators. Blending theory, storytelling, and performative methodologies, the channel turns research into accessible, visual learning experiences for all.
description_zh: Education Inqilab is a digital research YouTube channel hosting video essays to decolonise education, explore social justice, and empower creators. title_zh: 在工作中拥抱人性
description_en: The final service design outcome, BEING HUMAN is an internal workplace communication platform that helps creative organisations build a psychologically safe workplace for their employees to share their challenges and support each other.The platform allows employees to collaborate productively and supportively in day-to-day communications including new employee onboarding sessions, team meetings and regular check-ins.
description_zh: The final service design outcome, BEING HUMAN is an internal workplace communication platform that helps creative organisations build a psychologically safe workplace for their employees to share their challenges and support each other.The platform allows employees to collaborate productively and supportively in day-to-day communications including new employee onboarding sessions, team meetings and regular check-ins.

title_en: Embracing Openness: Rethinking Form and Structure in Music Composition and Performance
title_zh: 拥抱开放：重新思考音乐创作和表演的形式和结构
description_en: Chen Wang Cyber Maze is a 6-minute experimental sound piece exploring AI-human collaboration in music. Blending human vocals with AI-generated outputs, Chen Wang creates a layered, non-linear sonic maze. The work challenges authorship, merging emotional expression with algorithmic creativity, and reflects on technology's role in shaping artistic identity and the future of music. Contact Chenchenmusic0822@gmail.com
description_zh: Chen Wang Cyber Maze is a 6-minute experimental sound piece exploring AI-human collaboration in music. title_en: Embroidered Identity by Layla Al Idrissi
title_zh: Layla Al Idrissi的刺绣身份
description_en: "Embroidered Identity" explores the relationship between culture and self-identity by looking at traditional Palestinian embroidery through a contemporary lens. Passed down from mother to daughter, intricate motifs and symbols evolve with each generation, illustrating the power of embracing heritage to create a new sense of self.
description_zh: "Embroidered Identity" explores the relationship between culture and self-identity by looking at traditional Palestinian embroidery through a contemporary lens. Passed down from mother to daughter, intricate motifs and symbols evolve with each generation, illustrating the power of embracing heritage to create a new sense of self.

title_zh: 空商店
description_en: "Empty Store" is a collaborative AR immersive experience that merges physical reality with digital assets to tell a story about my experience in London, the challenges I encountered, and how I overcame them to feel confident and creative in this city. This project strongly focuses on digital fashion, where beautiful, colourful, and energetic garments made by 3D fashion artists represent my journey of overcoming daily difficulties.
description_zh: "Empty Store" is a collaborative AR immersive experience that merges physical reality with digital assets to tell a story about my experience in London, the challenges I encountered, and how I overcame them to feel confident and creative in this city. This project strongly focuses on digital fashion, where beautiful, colourful, and energetic garments made by 3D fashion artists represent my journey of overcoming daily difficulties.

title_en: Entering the Outland: How Can Cross-Media Miniature Installations Synthesise Creative Spaces to Preserve Hakka Memories?
title_zh: 进入外域：跨媒体微型装置如何合成创意空间以保留客家记忆？
description_en: Xinyu Li Vanishing Paths simulates the disordered and decayed state of Hakka dwellings, evoking their structural disintegration. Developed through Xinyu Li’s autoethnographic research, it draws on satellite maps, genealogies, and architectural field notes. By incorporating recycled materials, Vanishing Paths transfers the remains of one set of ruins into another, weaving new narratives into spaces left behind. Contact Xinyunaoqi71@gmail.comWebsite
description_zh: Xinyu Li Vanishing Paths simulates the disordered and decayed state of Hakka dwellings, evoking their structural disintegration. Developed through Xinyu Li’s autoethnographic research, it draws on satellite maps, genealogies, and architectural field notes. By incorporating recycled materials, Vanishing Paths transfers the remains of one set of ruins into another, weaving new narratives into spaces left behind. Contact Xinyunaoqi71@gmail.comWebsite

title_en: Entering the Outland: How Can Cross-Media Miniature Installations Synthesise Creative Spaces to Preserve Hakka Memories?
title_zh: 进入外域：跨媒体微型装置如何合成创意空间以保留客家记忆？
description_en:
Xinyu Li Jinshi was the highest honorary degree in the imperial examinations of ancient China. Within clan culture, a Jinshi title was often preserved in the ancestral hall as a symbol of family honour, its name meaning “one moves forward.” For Hakka communities today, that promise feels distant, shaped instead by retreat and decline. 

A dust-veiled Jinshi plaque still hangs in the clan hall, keeping vigil over empty homes. In its stillness, it perseveres as people drift away, leaving the village behind, scattering in search of a different way forward.Contact Xinyu naoqi71@gmail.com Website | Instagram
description_zh:
Xinyu Li Jinshi was the highest honorary degree in the imperial examinations of ancient China. Within clan culture, a Jinshi title was often preserved in the ancestral hall as a symbol of family honour, its name meaning “one moves forward.” For Hakka communities today, that promise feels distant, shaped instead by retreat and decline. 

A dust-veiled Jinshi plaque still hangs in the clan hall, keeping vigil over empty homes. In its stillness, it perseveres as people drift away, leaving the village behind, scattering in search of a different way forward.Contact Xinyu naoqi71@gmail.com Website | Instagram

title_zh: Janice Quiñonez的《Estamos Aquí》
description_en: Estamos Aqui, 2023 (We're here) is an exploration of indigenous womanhood in Bolivia; Cholita culture. Inspired by visits between Bolivia and the UK, the series became a way of expanding my relationships with the ancestry of Andean heritage and connecting with its safe-keepers both in Bolivia and in South London. It shows how women thrive despite living in a country where machismo and racism rule daily. It is a celebration of women, of the Bolivian culture and my connection with it.

It shows how women thrive despite living in a country where machismo and racism rule daily. It is a celebration of women, of the Bolivian culture and my connection with it.

title_en: Ethereal Whispers - Weather of Hyde Park
title_zh: 飘逸的低语-海德公园的天气
description_en: Guo presents Ethereal Whispers - Weather of Hyde Park, a body of research inspired by a walk through Hyde Park. Here, Guo positions the rustling leaves, trees and wind as musicians - allowing listeners a multi-sensory experience from a non-human perspective.

title_en: Evaluating the classical and medieval receptions in Genshin Impact through the transcultural study lens
title_zh: 通过跨文化研究镜头评估Genshin Impact的古典和中世纪接待
description_en:
Hanjun ShiGenshin Impact is an open world video game inviting players to explore different environments, including those based on classical and medieval eras. This exhibit presents photography and zines examining cultural representation in the game and how it acts against the ceaseless flow of time that can banish cultures into obscurity. The works on show hope to archive our daily lives—past, present, and future—and understand how our own memories interact with history. Contact Hanjun h.shi0320221@arts.ac.uk 
Website

title_en: Experiencing Social Justice Through the Senses
title_zh: 通过感官体验社会正义
description_en: This project explored how emotional and sensory experiences could reshape social justice reflection in educational contexts. Starting with a question — how do we visualise transformation? — Our team investigated alternatives to traditional evaluation methods, such as forms and surveys.

title_en: Going Anywhere: Creating Graphic Autobiographies from an Autistic Perspective
title_zh: 去任何地方：从自闭症的角度创作平面自传
description_en: Wang Qianyu Going Anywhere is part of Wang Qianyu’s series of graphic autobiographies exploring autism, comics, and the materiality of books. Based on her experiences visiting a city on different occasions, she layers transparent paper, tracing shifting perceptions, creating a tactile interplay between memory, place, and the act of drawing. Contact Wangq.wang1220232@arts.ac.uk

Readers are invited to pick it up and explore. Contact Qianyuq.wang1220232@arts.ac.uk

title_en: Grounded Digital: Embodied Interventions in the Ontology of Digital Anxiety
title_zh: 接地数字：数字焦虑本体论中的具体干预措施
description_en:
SungHoon Song This installation explores how digital signals can create a mode of embodiment. Viewers are invited to step inside this space where you can feel a simulation of the blowing of the wind. SungHoon Song explores how the experience of being is prompted by phenomena and the subsequent potential of digital art. Contact SungHoon lookomelette@gmail.com 
Website | Instagram

title_en: History is but a Sullen Memorabilia by Travis Yu
title_zh: 历史不过是Travis Yu的闷闷不乐的纪念品
description_en: History is but a Sullen Memorabilia is a body of work consisting of radio broadcasts, performance, sound recordings, writings and film. Comprises of Swan Song, Nobody Knows, Four Concrete Walls and Mirror, the portfolio establishes a wide range of vocabularies to transcribe the embodiment of sound and listening through the artist's choice of creative expressions.Concerned about imagining sound objects as memorabilia we hold onto as time goes on, Travis elaborated on their complex relationship with their heritage as well as the environment they found themselves in the present; the juxtaposed and the correlated, all melt together into organised sound and all kinds of movements.
description_zh: History is but a Sullen Memorabilia is a body of work consisting of radio broadcasts, performance, sound recordings, writings and film. Comprises of Swan Song, Nobody Knows, Four Concrete Walls and Mirror, the portfolio establishes a wide range of vocabularies to transcribe the embodiment of sound and listening through the artist's choice of creative expressions.Concerned about imagining sound objects as memorabilia we hold onto as time goes on, Travis elaborated on their complex relationship with their heritage as well as the environment they found themselves in the present; the juxtaposed and the correlated, all melt together into organised sound and all kinds of movements.

title_zh: 达维妮娅·克拉克（ Davinia Clarke ）的
description_en: This collection discusses the memories and stories we share in our everyday lives. It draws on the themes of migration, places and identity. Documented through the lens of my hybrid identity as a third generation child of the Jamaican diaspora but born in Britain. It seeks to reconnect through the feeling of being disconnected.
description_zh: This collection discusses the memories and stories we share in our everyday lives. It draws on the themes of migration, places and identity. Documented through the lens of my hybrid identity as a third generation child of the Jamaican diaspora but born in Britain. It seeks to reconnect through the feeling of being disconnected.

title_en: Knotting - a Sensual, Conceptual and Socio-political Encounter

description_en: Lynda Beckett A fold shapes the world through its body, its organs, and the influence of other bodies. The soul reflects what happens in the body and seems to suffer. Yet, the fold generates perceptions from within. Do external forces truly act upon it, or is this a deeper question of causality? – in-between 2025 Contact Lyndalbeckett27@gmail.comWebsite

title_zh: 认识你自己
description_en:
The aim of this service is to reduce the risk of burnout among grassroots staff in Chinese state-owned enterprises (SOEs) by increasing their sense of accomplishment. 

The service supports SOEs to help their staff to develop a mindset through mentoring, regular follow-up and adjustment so that they are constantly motivated to take action, gain fulfilment from their work and ultimately reduce or avoid burnout.
description_zh:

The service supports SOEs to help their staff to develop a mindset through mentoring, regular follow-up and adjustment so that they are constantly motivated to take action, gain fulfilment from their work and ultimately reduce or avoid burnout.

title_zh: 地球上的最后一天
description_en: Through a series of evocative photographs, Yueqian Hu depicts apocalyptic visions as they appear in her mind, confronting themes of moral dilemmas, the essence of survival, and the profound psychological shifts triggered by catastrophe.
description_zh: Through a series of evocative photographs, Yueqian Hu depicts apocalyptic visions as they appear in her mind, confronting themes of moral dilemmas, the essence of survival, and the profound psychological shifts triggered by catastrophe.

description_en: Lavly is the story of a Bengali migrant mother who moved to East London in the 80s, with the intention of living better and successful lives here in London. This project follows her journey taking the audience through many different eras, beginning in Bangladesh. Showing significant installations to commemorate the joys of what life was like in Bangladesh during the 70s. Followed by the harsh reality for a South Asian migrating to the fearful streets in East London during the 80s, the constant anxiety of becoming a victim to the violent crimes against those of colour. The journey then moves into a homely set, Lavly's home. A home where the audience sees the vivid image of what many Bengali homes looked like during the early 2000s.  This film tells the story of Lavly, our mother, as she motivates the audience to follow their own dreams as this life is filled with many possibilities. Referencing the events of her own life, she tells the audience her journey from Bangladesh, to now, a

title_en: Listening to Power: How can Listening and Drawing Enhance Critical Education Practice?
title_zh: 倾听力量：倾听和绘画如何增强批判性教育实践？
description_en: Miriam Elgon Located within the site of the UK Supreme Court, the highest appeal court in the UK, Miriam Elgon’s research develops a critical education methodology combining listening and drawing. Onsite sketches followed by transformations through drawing and print, the works here are a listening after or reverberation from the original events witnessed.  Contact Miriamcontact@miriamelgon.comWebsite | Instagram

title_en: Memory and remembrance of Romania’s 1980s everyday life under dictatorship, beyond the lens. Counter-narratives to recollection and reclamation of the past
title_zh: 对罗马尼亚20世纪80年代独裁统治下的日常生活的记忆和回忆，超越了镜头。对过去的回忆和回收的反叙述
description_en: Andreea Elle Vas Andreea Elle Vas invites you to step into the ‘square mile’ of her childhood under dictatorship: places that retain layers of memory, stories evoked through walking practice and embodied remembrance. Back in 1980s Bucharest, Andreea couldn't tell you her story, or even communicate to a foreigner without reporting it to the Securitate. Join this act of retrospective resistance and dissent on behalf of millions of children who suffered from state abuse in Romania – and for the millions that tragically still do, across the world today. Contact Andreeaa.paciu0220231@arts.ac.ukWebsite | Instagram

title_en: Messy Business: Deconstructing and Reformulating Educational Standardisations Through Artist-Teacher Praxis
title_zh: 混乱的业务：通过艺术家-教师实践解构和重构教育标准化
description_en: Adam SmithThis self-directed learning space reimagines education as temporary, material, and participatory. Focused around a colouring-book style poster triptych, it invites learning through images, tools, bodies, and space. Participation is warmly welcomed—through colouring, working with the accompanying worksheets, or following along with the Miezarute Art School instructional videos. In this post-human classroom, making slowly, experimenting, and engaging in collective authorship is encouraged.   Contact Adam info.miezarute@gmail.com Website | Instagram

title_en: New Ears for New Noise: How Sound Art Interrogates Smart City Wireless Density as Pollution
title_zh: 新噪音的新耳朵：声音艺术如何将智慧城市无线密度视为污染
description_en: Fari Bradley Addressing our diminishing right to protest, Fari Bradley's video installation PSA (Public Service Announcement) dissects what it is like to make oneself audible and visible in an increasingly surveilled society. Via this and works sewn in a hand-stitched vernacular, Bradley asks where we invest our attention, and our trust in the attention-depleting urban landscapes we must navigate today. Contact FariWebsite

description_en: Rontgen Gu's work explores the interconnectedness between all things. Northchurch Forest, based on two years of firsthand daily observations, seeks to witness the quiet process of nature reclaiming the local environment. The works, printed on a composite of soil, explore the relationship between urban development and nature.

title_en: Otherness: Creating heterotopic narrative public art by exploring identity, community, and space through site-specific interventions as a transnational researcher
title_zh: 异质性：作为跨国研究者，通过特定地点的干预措施探索身份、社区和空间，创造异域叙事公共艺术
description_en:
Mo Zhou Exploring voice, distance, and cross-cultural public memory, ‘Between the Lines’ engages with the familiar yet intimate medium of the telephone. Asking visitors to both listen and speak, Mo Zhou engages with otherness, identity, and community. Pick up the receiver to hear Mother’s greeting in hometown dialect—home carried across distance. Through the British phone, visitors are invited to “call home” and leave a message, shifting personal memory into a shared public narrative.  

All voice recordings are anonymous and collected solely as PhD research material and ethics-approved artistic research. Contact Mo Instagram

title_zh: 通过具体知识进行表演
description_en: Secil Yatan Gul  This work explores the relationship between movement and spatial representation. Through drawing, Secil Yatan Gul translates internal embodied knowledge into lines on paper. The representation functions as traces of bodies’ experience and imagination, recording shifts between internal embodied knowledge and space. Moving across different scales and modes of representation, the work examines how spatial knowledge is generated through movement practice, and how drawing can both document and re-activate that experience. This work is part of Secil Yatan Gul’s ongoing PhD thesis supervised by Dr. Jane Madsen and Prof. Vida L. Midgelow. Contact Secil s.yatangul0320241@arts.ac.uk

title_en: Photographic enquiries into contemporary conceptualisations of narrative self
title_zh: 对叙事自我的当代概念化的摄影探究
description_en:
Crispin Day Humans are essentially storytelling organisms, our sense of self composed of storied creations; fusions of memories, feelings, and experiences. ‘Tales of Unseen Stories’ explores the complex relationship between archival images and the fictive, speculative imaginaries of narrative self. Drawing from a personal archive inherited from an enigmatic uncle, the work reflects on the uncertainties and gaps within both the photographic and the narrative self. Contact Crispin crispinsmday@gmail.com 
Website | Instagram

title_en: Photography, Memory and Identity: the Challenges of Reconstructing the Visual Traces of Late Socialist 1980s Romania
title_zh: 摄影、记忆与身份：重建20世纪80年代末罗马尼亚视觉痕迹的挑战
description_en: Andreea Elle Vas Taking photos against the official narrative could have landed you in prison. Tales from the Silk Factory explores how, between family albums and propaganda archives, oral histories can help restore and preserve memory. Artist turned storyteller of her generation, Andreea Elle Vas shares the experience of children growing up under dictatorship in Bucharest’s neighbourhood Grivita. Contact Andreeaa.paciu0220231@arts.ac.ukWebsite | Instagram

title_en: Reimagining Authorship in the Age of AI: A Practice-led, Posthumanist Investigation into Human–AI Co-Creation in Visual Art
title_zh: 重新构想人工智能时代的作者身份：对视觉艺术中人类人工智能共同创造的实践主导的后人文主义调查
description_en:
Zihan Wang Challenging the boundary between subject and environment, this exhibit takes human anatomy and botanical forms, visualising a posthuman continuum. Working first by hand in ink and then feeding the “seeds” through AI software, Zihan Wang explores trans-corporeality, depicting the body not as a closed vessel but as an open node in an ecological network. The transition from black-and-white lines to digital hues marks a mutation from biological roots to an algorithmic future. Contact Zihanz.wang0720212@arts.ac.uk 
Instagram

title_zh: Cleo Amissah的Richmond Court
description_en: A documentary series that follows my first journey to Accra, Ghana. The project represents my evolving identity, and is a visual record of my heritage, relationships and the idea of returning that exists in diasporic communities. ﻿I began a project that explores my own identity politics, but is also a visual response to a relevant wider social issue of displacement.

description_en: The 'Sea It' Project utilizes QR codes on posters and interactive stickers to educate participants about the ocean's profound influence on everyday products. By scanning QR codes, users discover the marine ingredients in their belongings and affix corresponding stickers to items. These stickers serve as tangible reminders of the ocean's importance in their lives.

title_en: Spatial Sonics of a Contemporary Tree
title_zh: 当代树木的空间声音
description_en: Gareth Mitchell Memorialising the tree at their centre, fallen autumnal leaves of a pedunculate oak are heard circling and in mechanised, rhythmic configurations. Contrasting with surroundings of silence, the same tree and its leaves are subsequently encountered amid the cacophony and impingement of a modern, urban world. Contact Mitchellg.mitchell@lcc.arts.ac.uk

title_zh: 当代树木的空间声音
description_en:
Gareth Mitchell ‘Resonant Tree’ is a sound installation exploring the winter landscape of Brockwell Park in South London. A tree sheds leaves that gather then blow around its trunk. Twigs and branches snap underfoot as they are trudged over. The unseen streams of radio signals drift through the air, while rain lulls and replenishes. Traffic circles and aeroplanes arc overhead. Dimensions and time overlap and blur, energising and elucidating. Contact Garethg.mitchell@lcc.arts.ac.uk 
Website

title_en: Splintered Like the Body of a Goddess
title_zh: 像女神的身体一样碎裂
description_en: Through the mythology of Dewi Sri, the goddess of rice in Indonesia, I explore Javanese ecological knowledge, where life-sustaining plants are believed to emerge from her cadaver.
description_zh: Through the mythology of Dewi Sri, the goddess of rice in Indonesia, I explore Javanese ecological knowledge, where life-sustaining plants are believed to emerge from her cadaver.

title_zh: 由Gloria Corra缝制
description_en: This fashion animation delves into the abstract, using the movement of clothes as a symbolic thread that guides the viewer through an inner journey.

title_zh: 包容性未来的故事讲述工具包
description_en: Storytelling for inclusive futures toolkit is designed to enable the creation of a workshop that inspires students to engage meaningfully with social justice issues through storytelling and character design.
description_zh: Storytelling for inclusive futures toolkit is designed to enable the creation of a workshop that inspires students to engage meaningfully with social justice issues through storytelling and character design.

title_zh: 胀
description_en: Together, our team has developed a pilot concept for an iOS app called Swell. Designed to empower individuals to improve their holistic well-being, Swell helps users foster an intentional connection with water by incorporating the ocean into their daily lives. Based on research in behavior change methodologies, ecological principles, and the Blue Mind Theory which highlights the positive effects of water on human health, Swell was developed to “bring the ocean” to Gen Z populations residing in urban ocean cities who perceive a lack of access to coastal environments, while simultaneously expanding app users’ understanding of the ocean's critical role in furthering the health of our planet.

title_zh: 蓝色复兴
description_en: The Blue Resurgence is a 270 immersive experience that celebrates the findings of the 2020 study on the South Georgia blue whale resurgence. For the first time in 50 years, their numbers are rising once again. This is a celebration of successful climate activism and a sobering reminder of how easily we could lose this progress lest we regress.

This is a celebration of successful climate activism and a sobering reminder of how easily we could lose this progress lest we regress.

title_en: The Body in Algorithmic Colonialism: Artistic Inquiry into Emotion-AI and Embodied Affective Relations
title_zh: 算法殖民主义中的身体：对情感AI和具体情感关系的艺术探究
description_en:
Chunyi Shen This interactive installation examines how Emotion-AI, a subset of artificial intelligence, translates human emotions into standardised emotional categories. Tracing the gap between lived sensation and computational judgement, viewers are invited to interact with the work and reflect on moments of affective misrecognition. Instead of understanding the misrecognition as a technical failure, Chunyi Shen argues this dissonance is a form of political conditioning and algorithmic colonialism. This work-in-progress installation maps the algorithm–emotion loop, where enforced emotional “legibility” is used as a means of control. Contact Chunyi c.shen0620251@arts.ac.uk 
Website

title_en: The Fabric of the Field: Reweirding Rural and Remote Soundscapes
title_zh: 田野的构造：重新打造乡村和偏远的音景
description_en: Hector MacInnes Fulfilment Centre (2025) explores our diverse inhabitancy of the rural within the contemporary wellness-industrial complex. Hector MacInnes pulls on how the moments of location and dislocation between sound and image disrupt the prescription of rural futures by urban centres of population, and attend to an agency beyond the meeting of spiritual and material wants. Contact Hectorhec.macinnes@gmail.comWebsite | Instagram

description_zh: Thomas Keevil explores sustainable analogue photography in the LCC workshops, reimagining traditional techniques to link creative practice with climate justice and innovation.

title_en: The Guest Book by Aku Ratsula
title_zh: Aku Ratsula的留言簿
description_en: Raised in the United States by Finnish parents, my definition of home has always been stretched between two places. During the summers, we would visit Finland in time for the midsummer celebrations. Utopian memories from my childhood – playing in the forest, fishing and swimming – faded as I grew older and the distance between my family and I became more apparent. I felt pressure to choose between my American identity and Finnish heritage.In navigating what family and home mean to me, I embrace my unique relationships as they are. There was never a choice that needed to be made; the pressure that I once felt was all but an illusion.These images are set at the lakeside cabin where children play as I once did, and still do.

title_en: The Implicit Representation of Death in Contemporary Chinese Photography
title_zh: 中国当代摄影对死亡的隐含呈现
description_en: Ziyi Ren Building on field investigations at funeral parlours, funerary product shops, and a joss-paper factory in Sichuan Province, China, Ziyi Ren examines the complex, non-linear practices surrounding death. The works trace tensions that mark local unofficial funeral customs and commerce, using both photography and text as narrative methods. Visitors are invited to move through the installation. Contact Ziyiz.ren0520221@arts.ac.uk

Visitors are invited to move through the installation. Contact Ziyiz.ren0520221@arts.ac.uk

title_en: The Ways in Which my Practice Represents Family Life Through Film, Photography and Sculpture
title_zh: 我的实践通过电影、摄影和雕塑代表家庭生活的方式
description_en: Nilufar Nuthall Nilufar Nuthall’s research project explores family migration and uses the child walking in the landscape as a symbol for freedom of movement, a reference to the nomad and the settler. The child’s steps in the landscape are unsteady; they are learning about this new environment and connecting our past lives with the present. Contact NilufarWebsite | Instagram

title_en: Virtual Art Practices of Turning to Nature: Exploring Taoist and Deleuze Guattarian Philosophy in a Posthuman Era

description_en: Jasmine Kang My body dissolves from its confined limited dimensions and becomes a plane of consistency—an infinite surface embodying the universe. Geometry, once existing independently along the xyz axes at a fixed distance, intersected with this plane that is me. I can only receive this assemblage—strata upon strata of infinity. Contact JasmineInstagram | Rednote: 189123220

title_en: We Have Guests by Wilfred Carter
title_zh: 威尔弗雷德·卡特（ Wilfred Carter ）的
description_en: We Have Guests is a project that is providing a platform for Ukrainian Refugees to voice their experiences of the war, stories from times before, as well as their realities of sheltering in the UK.The project began in April 2022 after I began volunteering at local refugee support centres within Hampshire as my way of helping support Ukrainians coming into my local community. At these meetings, I began having conversations with Ukrainians who would tell me their stories, not just about the war but of their lives in Ukraine. It was then I knew what the project had to be, a platform for them to share these stories with the world.Fast forward a year and I have attended close to 100 support meetings and spoken in-depth with over 50 Ukrainian guests. At this stage, the project has taken the form of a book, which features 10 of these guests. The project is long-form so it is ongoing.

title_zh: 通过创造力实现福祉
description_en: “Well-being through Creativity” is a creative and mindful experience for people with emotions of anxiety which would allow them to control and minimise their symptoms. By fostering a sense of community and equipping people with imagination-based coping mechanisms, allowing people to manage and reduce these negative emotions. The process behind the design workshops enables people to share and express themselves through guidance and visual design. Using creativity to express imagination by drawing inspiration from observation, engaging in activities, and allowing ourselves to “play” can help process and control anxiety.

Using creativity to express imagination by drawing inspiration from observation, engaging in activities, and allowing ourselves to “play” can help process and control anxiety.

description_en: Edwige Seri Through Yes, to Me, Art It Is, Edwige Seri invites reflection on African philosophy through the visionary innovations of Frédéric Bruly Bouabré. Seri seeks to inspire Pan-African designers and digital creators to draw on their heritage, fostering social change through soft power, cultural identity, and artistic expression rooted in indigenous knowledge and traditions. Contact Edwigee.seri0820191@arts.ac.uk

title_en: 朝菌与椿 Virtual Art Practices of Turning to Nature: Exploring Taoist and Deleuze-Guattarian Philosophy in a Posthuman Era
title_zh: 转向自然的朝菌与椿虚拟艺术实践：探索后人类时代的道教和德勒兹-瓜塔尔哲学
description_en:
Jasmine Kang The great tree 椿 (Chun) possesses a trunk, growth rings, lineage, and history. The morning mushroom朝菌 (Chao Jun) falls into the soil, spreads, disappears, and returns. Standing with Chao Jun, this practice focuses on a life-temporarily that is always in the process of 'becoming'. 

Viewers are invited to interact with the work through gesture, subtly disturbing the environment. Through the entanglement of body, generative AI, code, and the medium, the work aims to shape an evolving virtual ecology. Contact Jasmine Rednote: 189123220 Instagram

description_en: I'm a Multi-Disciplinary designer with a passion for crafting experiences that have a positive impact on people's lives. Whether it's through user-centred UI/UX Design or innovative Service Design, I believe in using my skills to solve complex problems and improve the human experience. With a focus on empathy and critical thinking, I strive to design solutions that not only meet users' needs but delight and inspire them. I hold the view that empathy and critical thinking are the two most essential abilities for a designer. Through the years, I have learned how to balance empathy and critical thinking, and mastering this skill has helped me look beyond designing screens or products.As someone with expertise in both UI/UX and Service Design, I am skilled in integrating them to create cohesive and seamless experiences that drive customer loyalty. My in-depth understanding of the context in which digital touch-points are used and their role in the overall customer journey enables me to create interactions that align with customers' needs and expectations. By doing so, I can enhance the customer experience, differentiate the organisation from its competitors, and ultimately increase revenue and profitability. I have recently realised UX Design is more than just designing screens or products. It’s about the experience that comes with it. It’s about curating a memory in people’s minds through products/services that leave them with an everlasting impact. Since then, I have been learning how can one conduct research holistically without any bias and use critical design concepts to design solutions to problems that affect a wider mass. With every project, I aim to bring together art, science, and technology to create experiences that are intuitive, seamless, and memorable. Because to me, design is not just a job, it's an opportunity to make the world a better place, one experience at a time

description_en: I come from a trans-disciplinary design background with experience across various fields of design. I embarked on a journey to pursue my Master’s in Service design at the RCA with an intent of harmonising my varied skill sets to seamlessly integrate my design knowledge and drive meaningful impact in my professional endeavors.Design for me is to facilitate the act of amplifying aspirations, within your team, organization, users and stakeholders to co-create a ladder of impact. It inspired me to build my professional experiences working on real-life challenges in the field of fin-tech, gamification, health and well-being, rural business and social enterprises.My work at the Royal College of Art has empowered me to think, co-create, develop and validate a holistic service proposition by employing innovative research methodologies, conducting workshops, and feedback sessions.As I continue to build my practice as a service designer, my unwavering commitment is to consistently deliver impactful services by imbibing the humility to create an environment that facilitates change and empowers users, practicing empathy to embed within the user's psychology, and a willingness to explore a range of possibilities across disciplines that yield positive outcomes for communities and organisations.

description_en: Allison designs powerful (customer) service experiences for global companies. She currently is building companies to revolutionise the future of microgravity in-space and revolutionise the future of FemTech. Service design can act as a company's moral compass, and even more so, Allison believes companies can easily be reinvented based on how they use the materials they are given in service design. Futuristic designs that advance our human experience stand on the heels of those who believe in the vision and reimagination of the world around us. You will often find Allison's mind living in the future, thinking of ways to bridge the present. Her favourite line to use is, "Impossible is not as far as you think."

description_en: The Aesthetics of Smart TextilesThreads of Innovation: Music-Mind-TextileConfluence of Textile Art and Technological AdvancementsTextiles have long served as a means of covering and protecting the body, making them an ideal medium for empathy design thinking. Art and music, renowned for their therapeutic qualities, have the power to heal and inspire individuals. Through an innovative methodology, this initiative aims to bridge the gap between individuals and their environment, engaging them both mentally and physically. The integration of multifunctional devices into traditional textiles represents a significant leap forward in the field of smart textiles, combining the functionalities of textiles with electronic capabilities. This groundbreaking development transcends artistic concepts, offering a fresh perspective on creative expression. Smart textiles demonstrate exceptional flexibility, bendability, and adaptability, enabling diverse forms and shapes. Embedded fibre devices within the textiles introduce an interactive element, capable of detecting external stimuli and enriching the user experience.By employing sonic and neuro-aesthetics strategies in smart textiles, transformative possibilities emerge. This integration facilitates the production of functional smart lighting and display textiles on a larger scale, revolutionising areas infusing everyday objects, including textiles, with intelligent and interactive capabilities amplifies innovation and convenience. The harmonious fusion of music and textiles unlocks the therapeutic potential of both mediums. By integrating sound and neuro-aesthetic experiences, this pioneering approach benefits individuals from diverse backgrounds, promoting well-being and empowerment, particularly for those with neurodiversity. It provides a holistic encounter that transcends conventional artistic boundaries, immersing individuals in a transformative sensory experience.The incorporation of multi-sensory devices into traditional t

description_en: Hi, I'm Annie, an engineer-turned-designer passionate about creating a more equitable world. My work focuses on removing barriers for underserved communities.Through the course of my GID journey, I have had the pleasure of exploring design from multiple cultures across the world. This has brought an appreciation for designing mindfully with local cultures and more inclusivity into my practice.Presented below are my three latest projects that I conducted whilst in New York and back home in London. Reset is a facilitation tool for effective family Carepair dynamics. Smart home devices are used to create a calming and intimate environment at home and community-based insights are used to open important care topics. Reset's aim is to help Carepairs better understand and connect with each other, creating more opportunity for moments of joy.Pod is a continuous health monitor helping people with chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) better manage their symptoms. By combining ECG technology with conductive temporary tattoos, Pod aims to monitor a person’s vitals with medical grade recordings, whilst remaining comfortable to wear over a long time.Lafayette Gardens was a 7-week community led project within a social housing development in Brooklyn, New York. 'Mr Ty's Words of Wisdom' was created as a logbook and guide to help residents better access resources available to them. This project was created by the residents, for the residents.Ultimately, my design reflects a key belief, which is that everyone deserves care.Please get in touch if you would like to discuss current or potential future projects more!

My work focuses on removing barriers for underserved communities.Through the course of my GID journey, I have had the pleasure of exploring design from multiple cultures across the world. This has brought an appreciation for designing mindfully with local cultures and more inclusivity into my practice.Presented below are my three latest projects that I conducted whilst in New York and back home in London. Reset is a facilitation tool for effective family Carepair dynamics. Smart home devices are used to create a calming and intimate environment at home and community-based insights are used to open important care topics. Reset's aim is to help Carepairs better understand and connect with each other, creating more opportunity for moments of joy.Pod is a continuous health monitor helping people with chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) better manage their symptoms. By combining ECG technology with conductive temporary tattoos, Pod aims to monitor a person’s vitals with medical grade recordings, whilst remaining comfortable to wear over a long time.Lafayette Gardens was a 7-week community led project within a social housing development in Brooklyn, New York. 'Mr Ty's Words of Wisdom' was created as a logbook and guide to help residents better access resources available to them. This project was created by the residents, for the residents.Ultimately, my design reflects a key belief, which is that everyone deserves care.Please get in touch if you would like to discuss current or potential future projects more!

description_en: Where I am in 2022I am deeply passionate about integrating research and human understanding into the design process. My experience at RCA over the past two years has given me the tools to explore the intricate relationship between culture, society, and design. I have come to comprehend the profound impact they exert on one another, empowering me to develop meaningful solutions that address real-world challenges with purpose and empathy.RCA ShowcaseIn the RCA2023 show, I am giving you a sneak peek of two pieces I worked on during my time at the RCA: LimiSphere: Meals with a Purpose is a comprehensive system designed to prevent loneliness among men about to retire by promoting awareness and involvement in community projects; Re-Told a storytelling tool that helps younger generations document their families’ oral stories, whilst preserving their cultural heritage and recording dying dialects and languages.

description_en: I am an interdisciplinary artist and researcher who explores textiles as a web of embodied connections between land, bodies and climate change. Proposing new narratives to understand the different layers of knowledge that feed both contemporary and ancestral practices. I examine the aesthetics of indigenous cultures, power relations and interactions between ecologies and technologies, and the environmental impacts of extractive operations.My practice explores textiles in relation to sound, biomaterials, and electronic devices, all of which are essential elements in the production of speculative projects. These speculative projects explore the relationship between technology, culture, and nature to challenge and expand our understanding of ecological systems. In this sense, I see textiles as an interface that connects people to intercultural perspectives where matter is conceived in relation to technology and myth.My process is based on a holistic perspective that embodies indigenous knowledge to stimulate haptic imagination. Haptic imagination encompasses touching as media. Touch can create immediate and intimate relations with materials, bodies, landscapes, and other cultural thinking. Touching as a means of interaction augments emotions and memory. I am currently looking at pre-Columbian notations, namely tattoos, as a medium to open intercultural perspectives around technology. By creating mechanisms and tools, I am trying to bring contemporary relevance to ideas of healing, activism, and environmental issues.By combining technological tools and processes –such as robotics, open-source hardware, digital embroidery– with traditional practices –such as handloom, waist loom (traditional Peruvian technique), hand embroidery, natural materials lab, and engraving– my aim is to create patterns that link past and present, but also point to our dependence on the earth and our human limitations.

title_zh: Chenyue (Channy) Hong
description_en: A creative problem solver, open-minded explorer, free-thinking artist, who thrives on challenges and loves working alongside interdisciplinary teams to bring ideas to life.With a passion for pushing the boundaries of what's possible, I'm always on the hunt for the latest trends and insights, constantly looking for ways to innovate and improve. Communication is key to my work, and I use it to build strong relationships with stakeholders and keep my team aligned toward a common goal.My design approach involves keen observation and deep analysis, allowing me to uncover hidden gems within every trendy phenomenon and complex problem. This relentless pursuit of a unique angle enables me to craft innovative solutions that captivate and delight. My design philosophy revolves around constant engagement with people throughout the design process, ensuring my creations resonate on a profound level by immersing myself in the real world and listening intently to their needs and desires. During my time at the Royal College of Art, I gained extensive experience in service design, experience design, and strategy design. I was fortunate enough to work with The Big Issue Group on a project where we successfully launched a food stall service on the RCA Battersea campus.For my master's final year project, with a passion for nature and its transformative impact, I worked on exploring enhancing the working culture for remote workers, promoting crucial work-life balance, and cultivating an environment that nurtures well-being and productivity by integrating nature-inspired elements into company practices, ultimately fostering a healthier and happier remote workforce.

description_en: Extremely Odd, Completely IrrationalIf every blink is a frame, we experience and understand the world as a sequence of images. This makes creating visuals a way of constructing our reality and belief. We are in a generation filled with images that we have became numb to them, thinking we have seen it all. We are actively yearning, but passively looking. Visual experience is consumed carelessly, making the act of seeing powerless. ‘Extremely Odd, Completely Irrational’  is intended to bring a tender disruption, a soft pause to the loud and accelerating modern society in order to reimagine the potential of seeing.This rhizomatic series of experimentation does not answer but inquire. Tracing back to the intricate mechanism of visual perception and the idea of forming in craft making, ‘Extremely Odd, Completely Irrational’ is an exploration of the design thinking/method that merges the process of physical making with digital imagery. This ongoing research project explores the potential of visual language in abstraction through material voice and human action. The artist crafted visual experiences like constructing a piece of fabric. The human movement and unexpected material reactions bring uncertainty and new perspective to ordinary things, motivating the viewer to see beyond what is visible. The abstractions scrutinise the potential of visual narrative and material language through the tension of paradoxical dichotomy and the presence of absence. The unsettling ambiguity and enigmatic quietness prompt a closer and slower way of looking. The distressing yet comforting experience of ruminating on the work aims to create sublimity that prompts the audience to stop looking externally but explore internally, to stop dwelling in the known but to rediscover the unknown.

description_en: When I am designing, I consider the consumer at the forefront. Yes I accept we design for a consumer, but the user experience of a product is very important to me.As a designer I like to follow my hands and trust their intuition. Problems will be uncovered and solved as the making process continues. I trust the process of the previous design informing the next. The platform Design Through Making has allowed me to explore this iterative process strategically, whilst also allowing for naivety and mistakes. I am very consumer focussed when designing and consider how the product is interacted with whilst also instilling consumer needs into my designs. When designing, I consider the environment, but does not lead the design. When taking a design through to fruition - the product will aim to withstand length of time through durability and aesthetics.

description_en: I’m excited by functional design; constantly inspired by the potential to streamline and automate anything. To me, brilliant design means intention, intelligence and efficiency. I strive to create experiences that are smooth, delightful, and to an extent, unnoticed. My professional practice lies at the intersection of creativity and technology, and I am driven by the prospect of using technology to enrich our unpredictable world. I hope to continue exploring different methodologies of research, design, and making to challenge myself as an evolving creator.

description_en: Where am I?- I, suspended, am neither here nor there.Whose dream am I building? - Dreams, only the unattainable ones are called dreams. Not mine anyway, after all, I haven't dreamed for a long time.In the Green Fantasy project, an on-site interactive experience is created for second-generation migrant workers working in the construction industry in China. On the construction site, a green screen interactive installation made of debris netting, scaffolding structures and other site materials weaves an fantasy of success and reunion for them, exploring the plight of Career, Identity and family faced by the second generation of migrant construction workers and what do those mean to them.

description_en: SEBThe juxtaposition between stillness and movement, sharpness and softness. Within this exploration, I took a deep dive into the duality of my own identity, contemplating between staying still or running away.Military-inspired garments that typically convey notions of constraint and immobility was manipulated with soft lines and movements. Physicality, a symbol of youth, was infused into the patterns. I breathe new life into these garments, offering them a new perspective.Through hand-tailoring, a craft that I became obsessed with, I capture and document the conflict that unfolds during my journey. The repetitive and almost mechanical motions of tailoring have transformed into rituals that hold great significance for me. I wish to find peace and enlightenment in every stitch.

description_en: ‘Invisibility of gender minority’I blend green screen effect into garments to expand the realm of digital wear design. I wanted to challenge traditional making fashion material methods by introducing green screen effect. The idea was sparked after seeing a physical scene on a film set with endless options. I was working on a design for a service that would allow customers to design and customise their own name tags with 3D modelling, when I was thinking about how my material relates to my body, sexuality, and my gender identity. As a gender minority person, I would observe my body modification as a medium for identity; weaving multiple ideas to indicate gender society’s reality. Since the human body (material) does not have a green color, we use green as a green screen effect, which gives me a color that symbolizes infinity in the real world, hence the title "invisibility and infinity". Within it, I capture identity in green colour, exploring invisible transgender people in Korea and growing up as a queer in a small community. It is the materials evoking me to study and develop an infinite number of real-world materials; Tattooed vegetable tanned cow hide leather, translucent leather, Turkish green leather, silicone rubber, piercing and so on. I have tried not only repelled the methodological stereotype of fashion, but it lured in a whole of papers about the reality of transgender people in Korean society to realise the vision. Alongside vegetable tanned leather, multiple types of leather and tattoo ink, I tattooed with tattoo machine their edited body footage from the colours of green screen colour, ensuring that all artwork on leather was hand drawing.

description_en: In my work, I try to encourage speculation and criticality on behalf of the user: I believe design should enable people to exert agency over their experiences. I try to draw out the absurd qualities from everyday life to help people develop a greater understanding of the role design plays and the relationship between an object and its context.The projects shown here explore future possibilities in an attempt to understand the present. Tuning Conversations questions what social standards might look like when digital communication moves off-screen to act more like a physical conversation. Mallcraft asks players to express what they value in public space through a placemaking game. Studio for the End of the World uses AI and current news and innovation to imagine a fictional branding studio creating household brands of the future.

description_en: Through Avery's work, he seeks to engage viewers in critical conversations about the world they live in. He firmly believes that art has the remarkable ability to serve as a potent tool for social and political commentary. His aim, therefore, is to create compelling pieces that not only captivate the audience but also provoke deep reflection on their own beliefs and assumptions. By crafting immersive digital worlds, he invites viewers to actively explore and engage with his ideas.Every artwork Avery creates serves as an invitation to contemplate the intricate and multifaceted issues that shape our world. He wants his audience to delve into the depths of their thoughts, question the established norms, and challenge their preconceived notions. He strives to ignite a spark within each viewer, one that inspires them to examine their role in society and consider the impact of their actions.As an artist, Avery embraces the responsibility to foster dialogue and facilitate critical thinking through his creations. By sparking meaningful conversations and encouraging introspection, he hopes to contribute to a more conscious and empathetic society, one where individuals are not afraid to confront and question the complexities of the world around them.

description_en: Virtual Try-on With MobilityThe design concept focuses on virtual try-on technology, and integrates fashion brand with mobility. Inspired by Rick Owens' fashion design attitude of "Not care," which emphasizes self-expression and breaking free from norms.It combines drone delivery with mobility to create future business model that using drones with mobility, potentially allow for faster and more efficient delivery of clothes. The project is focused on providing a personalized and sustainable mobility solution that prioritizes self-expression and energy efficiency, providing flexibility and privacy for the user.

description_en:
Jade Rivalland is a creative designer from Nantes, France. Determined & passionate, she tackles global issues in her work, focusing on themes such as sustainability, smart tech & new experiences for the automotive industry. Jade's work carries forward-thinking concepts in order to challenge the human relationship to the future of mobility. Questioning the way we use products and vehicles, she is always wondering “what if?” to imagine what our future could be made of.

As cities grow bigger by the day, future urban life will be featured with diversity, sustainability, technology and mobility. While environmental concerns become increasingly urgent, we can expect the next generation to face new challenges, and sustainable practices are likely to become more widespread in the field of mobility. New models of (not so much anymore) futuristic cities are emerging, so how would mobility fit in those future urban areas , and what would be the needs of the 2040 urban population? How would that influence the way young people move around in existing European cities? At the same time, life will be surrounded by AI and other new technologies, creating a new balance between the on & off line, and younger users will have different expectations from the off line, the physical life. After the era of purity and minimalism, youth tends to want humaneness back in their life. How to create a new aesthetics, more human centered and user friendly ? For young people, often outside and hanging out in groups, the need to move within the city in essential. How can we meet the emerging needs of the next young generation to provide a dedicated mobility solution based on the urban lifestyle ?
